description_long: This shelter is a traditional, oval-shaped structure with a thatched roof made from straw or reeds, supported by a visible wooden frame. The roof is thick and layered, providing substantial coverage and insulation, and it extends beyond the walls to form a protective overhang. The shelter sits on a flat, earthen base, and one side is open, revealing an interior space with a central fire pit and some basic furnishings. The wooden support beams are arranged in a regular pattern, giving the structure stability and a rustic appearance. The entryway is wide and open, lacking a door, which allows easy access and ventilation. The overall design suggests a communal or gathering space, suitable for parks or outdoor urban environments.
description: An oval, thatched-roof shelter with a wooden frame and open entryway, revealing a communal interior. The structure features a thick, straw roof and a wide, accessible front.
description_view_0: The front view shows the thick, layered thatched roof and the wide, oval base, with the entryway not visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the sloped roof and the exposed wooden frame on one half, showing the structural supports.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the open side of the shelter, displaying the interior space, central fire pit, and wooden support beams.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again shows the sloped roof and the open framework, with the entryway partially visible.
attribute_roof_type: thick, oval thatched roof made from straw or reeds, with layered construction
attribute_entryway_design: wide, open passage without a door, framed by wooden beams
attribute_support_structure: wooden beams arranged in a regular, supportive framework
